content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN gears numbered 111 through NNN. For each valid iii, gear iii has AiA_iAi‚Äã teeth. In the beginning, no gear is connected to any other. Your task is to process MMM queries and simulate the gears' mechanism. There are three types of queries:


Type 1: Change the number of teeth of gear XXX to CCC.
Type 2: Connect two gears XXX and YYY.
Type 3: Find the speed of rotation of gear YYY if gear XXX rotates with speed VVV.
It is known that if gear iii is directly connected to gear jjj and gear iii rotates with speed VVV, then gear jjj will rotate with speed ‚àíVAi/Aj-V A_i / A_j‚àíVAi‚Äã/Aj‚Äã, where the sign of rotation speed denotes the direction of rotation (so minus here denotes rotation in the opposite direction). You may also notice that gears can be blocked in some cases. This happens when some gear would have to rotate in different directions. If a gear is connected to any blocked gear, it is also blocked. For example, if three gears are connected to each other, this configuration can not rotate at all, and if we connect a fourth gear to these three, it will also be blocked and not rotate.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The following MMM lines describe queries. Each of these lines begins with an integer TTT denoting the type of the current query.

If T=1T = 1T=1, it is followed by a space and two space-separated integers XXX and CCC.
If T=2T = 2T=2, it is followed by a space and two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.
If T=3T = 3T=3, it is followed by a space and three space-separated integers XXX, YYY and VVV.
content.outputFormat: For each query of type 3, print a single line containing two integers separated by a slash '/' ‚Äî the numerator and denominator of the rotation speed of the given gear expressed as an irreducible fraction (even if this speed is an integer), or 000 if the gear does not rotate at all.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For the first query of type 3, there are no connections between gears, so the answer is 000.
For the second query of type 3, we can calculate the rotation speed using the formula ‚àí3‚ãÖ68=‚àí94-3\cdot\frac{6}{8} = \frac{-9}{4}‚àí3‚ãÖ86‚Äã=4‚àí9‚Äã.
For the third query of type 3, we can use the formula twice, so the speed of the second gear is ‚àí10‚ãÖ78=‚àí354-10\cdot\frac{7}{8} = -\frac{35}{4}‚àí10‚ãÖ87‚Äã=‚àí435‚Äã, and the speed of the third gear is ‚àí(‚àí354)810=71-(-\frac{35}{4})\frac{8}{10} = \frac{7}{1}‚àí(‚àí435‚Äã)108‚Äã=17‚Äã.
For the last query of type 3, all gears are blocked.
